description: Koios pool_stake_snapshot for Cardano preview testnet — golden vector for issue #422 mark/set/go regression tests.
notes: Koios labels the SNAPSHOTS with the epoch in which they are ACTIVE. When the current epoch is 1268:
- 'Go'   (the stake-distribution used for rewards) carries epoch_no=1267
- 'Set'  (the stake-distribution used to elect this epoch's leaders) carries epoch_no=1268
- 'Mark' (the freshly-captured stake-distribution, live for the next epoch) carries epoch_no=1269
This matches Haskell's ss{Mark,Set,Go} shift convention: mark is the freshest, go is two epochs back.
Dugite stores the same three snapshots with the same rotation semantics, see crates/dugite-ledger/src/state/epoch.rs recompute_snapshot_pool_stakes.

6954ec11cf7097a693721104139b96c54e7f3e2a.comment: SAND has 3 stable delegators and no active_stake change across the window. Verifies that dugite's mark/set/go report the same value when the underlying stake is stable, and that zero-drift does not hide the #418 regression.

totals.comment: active_stake is Koios's epoch-wide total for the respective snapshot. Between set and mark the drift is approximately 0.0215% (~270 million lovelace out of 1.26 trillion). Between go and set it is approximately 0.316%. Any live-clobber regression (#418) would collapse all three totals to the same value.
